title: podman hangs with deadlock
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:

 **kind bug**

[//]: # **kind feature**

**Description**

podman deadlocks on /var/lib/containers/storage/storage.lock

```
-bash-4.2$ ps -ef | grep podman
username   5343 29530  0 20:23 pts/1    00:00:00 grep --color=auto podman
root     13804     1  0 Sep27 ?        00:00:08 /usr/bin/podman --root /var/lib/containers/storage --runroot /var/run/containers/storage --log-level error --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /var/run/libpod --storage-driver overlay container cleanup 62bedf7f6d3a55020e7f314f401769281ef5b16c222d1753b0281590b48f63bb
root     14038 13701  0 Sep27 ?        00:02:25 /usr/bin/podman wait container_1537988606812_0012_01_000005
root     15355     1  0 Sep27 ?        00:00:07 /usr/bin/podman --root /var/lib/containers/storage --runroot /var/run/containers/storage --log-level error --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /var/run/libpod --storage-driver overlay container cleanup cf2ac277eb9a5b85e1b78ef088a5fff8ae41ac29639d27f77ade427211378d87
root     15359     1  0 Sep27 ?        00:00:07 /usr/bin/podman --root /var/lib/containers/storage --runroot /var/run/containers/storage --log-level error --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /var/run/libpod --storage-driver overlay container cleanup 79181a9932ae72ae4fe7b8377ab92cc57d1651dcd7e47dca4596b29b3787e934
root     15543 15298  0 Sep27 ?        00:01:51 /usr/bin/podman wait container_1537988606812_0012_01_000011
root     15546     1  0 Sep27 ?        00:00:08 /usr/bin/podman --root /var/lib/containers/storage --runroot /var/run/containers/storage --log-level error --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /var/run/libpod --storage-driver overlay container cleanup eb569521b8838b1336e6a23428c3f033ee981f7113d5ccc50967f0775dfae5dc
root     15564 15297  0 Sep27 ?        00:01:56 /usr/bin/podman wait container_1537988606812_0012_01_000017
root     15769 15404  0 Sep27 ?        00:01:56 /usr/bin/podman wait container_1537988606812_0012_01_000016
root     22399  9698  0 08:01 ?        00:00:00 [podman] <defunct>
```
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Launch several containers at the same time

2. wait for pod man to hang


**Output of `podman info`:**

```
$ sudo podman info
host:
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.9.4-1537914470.gitf4e2810f.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 51dcc3ad101da1c64efd9d49a1a0911242beac29-dirty'
  MemFree: 9827209216
  MemTotal: 16557703168
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-37.rc5.dev.gitad0f525.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: host01.example.com
  kernel: 3.10.0-862.9.1.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 702h 19m 22.38s (Approximately 29.25 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 5
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```
Attaching a file with the strace and pstack since it is large.

[podman_deadlock_modified.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/2435498/podman_deadlock_modified.txt)

comments.body: Can you get the output of `lslocks` during the deadlock?
Appears to be `podman wait` racing against `podman cleanup` - how exactly are you reproducing this, running a loop that makes a detached container and then waits for it?

```
-bash-4.2$ lslocks
COMMAND           PID   TYPE SIZE MODE   M      START        END PATH
lvmetad           460  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /run/netns
osqueryd         1474  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
osqueryd         1474  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay

java            12368  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
nslcd            1327  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /run/netns
dockerd-current  2330  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
nessusd         31866  POSIX   0B READ   0 1073741826 1073742335 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
nessusd         31866  POSIX   0B READ   0        128        128 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
java            12368  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
java            12368  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
yakl-client     10832  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
falcond           655  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /run/netns
master           1905  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
master           1905  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
(unknown)          -1 OFDLCK   0B READ   0          0          0 /dev
supervise        9316  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
ykeykeyd         9317  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
jsvc            16188  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
java            12368  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
java            12368  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
podman          32595  POSIX   0B WRITE* 0          0          0 /run/netns
podman          32736  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /run/netns
atd              2247  POSIX   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /run/netns
supervise        9315  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
multilog         9318  FLOCK   0B WRITE  0          0          0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
```
Hm. It looks like Nessus has grabbed a POSIX lock on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay. C/storage uses flock, but it's possible it's still interfering with Podman.

The way I get it to deadlock quickly is by running a Hadoop job that spawns JVMs that touch a lot of memory and try to make the VM swap. This is a test that I have been running on docker to get docker to fail with the containerd vs dockerd out of sync issue.

I'm putting money on this being the JVM and/or Nessus POSIX locks on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay interfering with containers/storage

@nalind Any thoughts?

Hm. The locks in question aren't mandatory, though.

One Podman is stuck waiting for /var/run/netns, which sounds like the CNI plugins?
@unxfrek Are you spawning the JVMs in containers, or are they just a way to make the host run out of memory and force swapping?
eliminate the networking component with --network=none ?
@unxfrek any chance you could share the Hadoop job or otherwise provide a reproducer?  It would be handy for our diagnosis and future testings.

They've been able to turn off nessus on the VMs that are being used to test podman and rerun the tests. However, they still are able to generate a deadlock on each VM running the same test. I've attached logs for all 3 of these VMs. Two things are particularly interesting:

1. The deadlock only happens on `podman ps -a`. I can still run containers via `podman run` perfectly fine. This is an upgrade over the previous deadlocks that I was seeing where everything related to podman would hang.

2. The straces of the `podman ps -a` on each VM seem to stick at different points in the code. But for each VM, they always stick during those same pieces of the code across different runs of `podman ps -a`

I'll see about a reproducer.

- Jacob
[podman_deadlock_no_nessus_10_5.3.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/2460402/podman_deadlock_no_nessus_10_5.3.txt)
[podman_deadlock_no_nessus_10_5.2.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/2460403/podman_deadlock_no_nessus_10_5.2.txt)
[podman_deadlock_no_nessus_10_5.1.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/2460404/podman_deadlock_no_nessus_10_5.1.txt)

Hey all, I'm the hadoop dev who @unxfrek is reporting this issue for. Let me give some more details and hopefully we can iterate more quickly with me responding directly here. 

A little background on how I'm reproducing this; I have a VM-based Hadoop cluster with 3 worker nodes (this is where podman is run), each with 16GB of memory and all running RHEL 7.5. I've been keeping up to date with the latest podman nightly releases from RH, but I always post the version along with my outputs. Currently I'm running with podman-0.9.4-1537914470.gitf4e2810f.el7.x86_64. The output I've attached below all corresponds to the text file podman_deadlock_no_nessus_10_5.3.txt that @unxfrek attached in the previous comment. I still have this deadlock sitting, intact, so I can get whatever output you'd like from it or any of the other nodes.

Here is the specific job that I run to get podman stuck. Usually it happens on the first run, but sometimes it takes 2 or 3 times. This is running on our own custom hadoop release, so it might not reproduce with an Apache build. Podman also isn't actually supported in Apache Hadoop yet, so we've had to make some small changes to the docker code just to get this to work at all. 
 
```
$HADOOP_PREFIX/bin/hadoop jar $HADOOP_PREFIX/share/hadoop/mapreduce/hadoop-mapreduce-client-jobclient-*-tests.jar sleep -Dmapreduce.map.java.opts="-Xms3500M -Xmx3500M -XX:+AlwaysPreTouch" -Dmapreduce.map.memory.mb=4000 -m 200 -r 1 -mt 1 -rt 1
```

I think your early assertion is correct that this is a deadlock between podman container cleanup and podman wait. Giving a root output from lslocks, it shows that podman wait is holding the write lock on /run/libpod/lock/039c73bae701c7fc6ecd3dfe2b52175f5c4e1406dc1b98677078e2a0218e5fe4 and podman container cleanup is trying to acquire that same lock. The interesting part to me is that it looks like /usr/bin/runc state is deadlocked under the hood. It looks like there's even some runc commands that were never cleaned up from the last few times I produced this deadlock scenario. In the output below, you can see that the runc state process 5784 has 5046 as its parent, which is the podman wait command. However, when I run the exact same /usr/bin/runc state command now it completes successfully and doesn't hang at all. Not sure why it wouldn't get stuck on the same lock. 


@ericbadger Thanks - that's very thorough!

This is seeming like `runc state` getting stuck somewhere and not returning, causing us to get caught in a critical section.

We can fix this locally by adding a timeout on our call to runc - 10 seconds, maybe? - after which we kill runc and abandon the operation.

That's just treating the symptoms, though. I'm betting the root cause here is a runc bug causing it to occasionally hang when doing `runc status` calls. Can you get a more detailed `ps` of the `runc` processes (something that shows process state, `ps aux` should be fine) and maybe try and strace, see if it's trying to grab a lock or similar?

@mheon sorry for not including the strace/pstack in the last comment. I had the output, just forgot to include it in the comment. Anyway, it's stuck waiting on some futex. Not sure where to go from there on the debugging front.

```
[root@hostname1 overlay-containers]# strace -fp 5784
strace: Process 5784 attached
futex(0x562902da4360, FUTEX_WAIT, 2, NULL^Cstrace: Process 5784 detached
 <detached ...>
```

```
[root@hostname1 overlay-containers]# pstack 5784
#0  0x000056290261e1c3 in runtime.futex ()
#1  0x00005629025eb842 in runtime.futexsleep ()
#2  0x0000562902da4360 in __bss_start ()
#3  0x0000000200000000 in ?? ()
#4  0x0000000000000000 in ?? ()
```

@mheon why do we need to run runc status?  Is there some piece of data that we need from runc that could cause this issue?  Could their be a runc on a container as it exits that causes this?
I think a timeout of 1 second would be better then waiting 10 seconds.

@rhatdan We need to ping runc to see if the container is alive or dead, so I don't think we can get away with getting rid of it (though moving to cleanup processes for DB updates would substantially reduce the amount of them we need)
I think this is a runc bug of some sort, though. It seems to be stuck in a futex, so it could be a race condition

Can't we rely on conmon to have told us that by dropping an exit code?  If their is no exit code then we figure the container is alive.  Maybe we add a flag to podman ps -a --fullcheck or something that will check with the container runtime.

Is there any additional information or testing that I can do to help out with this? I'd really like to get to the bottom of what's going on here so that we can deploy podman at scale. I just don't know the internals of podman or runc :)

I'll see about getting a temporary fix in for this this week, using a timeout on runc.

I'm still confident the underlying cause is a runc bug, though not exactly sure where. @mrunalp Anything debugging-wise we can do to chase this down?

@mheon Try stracing runc to see where it is hanging. Also, like @rhatdan said, you shouldn't have to call runc state frequently. CRI-O uses inotify on conmon created exit file to know when a container has stopped.
Part of me wonders if this isn't related to https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1681 - we could be seeing a lot of defunct runc processes accumulating without that PR, especially with wait
